qa_pairs.question: When an updated Alliance CCV is submitted that is older than 90 days, how are publications that are new to the CCV identified, and what must be done with them?
qa_pairs.answer: When an updated Alliance CCV is successfully uploaded, any new publications that have been added (compared to the ones on file) will be marked with a red border. To complete the reporting process and submit your application, you must review and address all these newly marked publications.

qa_pairs.question: How do you indicate that a new publication in your Alliance CCV was *not* enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: To mark a new publication as not enabled by computational resources, you must click on it twice. The first click will show a checkmark, indicating it was enabled by resources. The second click on the same box will remove the checkmark, signifying that the new publication was not enabled by computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to previously reported publications when an updated Alliance CCV is successfully uploaded?
qa_pairs.answer: When an updated Alliance CCV is successfully uploaded, the system will display the list of publications included in the most recently submitted CCV, remembering how you previously marked them as enabled by computational resources.
